dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, everything's falling apart. The settlement found out what we've been doing.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} saw you heading toward the {enemy_type} camp and told everyone. The guards are organizing a raid to wipe them out at dawn: {quest_focus} means nothing to them. We have until then to gather enough {quest_item} to convince the {enemy_type} to relocate somewhere safe. {quest_stakes} if blood gets spilled.
dialogueChunks.outro: I started this. {quest_threat} is about to end in massacre unless we act before dawn.

playerResponses.text: Then we move fast. I'll get the supplies before dawn.
You should have spoken up sooner. But I'll try to stop this.
You chose the wrong side. I'm staying out of this.

dialogueChunks.intro: This is worse than I feared, {player_name}. Much worse.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} took your visit as a sign of alliance. Now they're coming to {location} expecting friendship, but the guards will see armed creatures approaching and assume attack. You need to intercept them {location_hint} before they walk into a bloodbath. Take {quest_item} as a gesture of good faith. {quest_focus} could be destroyed by a simple misunderstanding.
dialogueChunks.outro: Two groups who could live in peace are about to destroy each other. {quest_threat} continues unless someone stands between them.

playerResponses.text: I'll intercept them. Nobody dies today.
Caught between two sides and trusted by neither. Wonderful.
Let them sort it out themselves. This isn't my war.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, something terrible has happened at the {enemy_type} camp.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A rogue faction killed their own elder: the one who wanted peace. Hardliners have taken control and they're planning revenge. {target_npc} says the remaining moderates fled {location_hint}. They're the only ones who can calm the war party. We need to find them and bring them {quest_item} to prove we still want peace. {quest_focus} hangs by a thread.
dialogueChunks.outro: The window for peace is closing fast. {quest_threat} wins if the moderates lose hope.

playerResponses.text: I'll find the moderates. Peace is worth fighting for.
Their own people killed the peacemaker? This might be hopeless.
Internal politics of monsters isn't my problem.
